title: Java Structured Concurrency
description: Run subtasks as a single unit of work with StructuredTaskScope.
learningGoals: Fork concurrent subtasks and join them together with StructuredTaskScope.
Read each Subtask result with get() after a successful join.
Note: structured concurrency is a preview feature in Java 26, enabled with --enable-preview.

completionChecks.prompt: What does scope.join() coordinate in this structured-concurrency lesson?
choices: It converts every task into a virtual thread factory.
It serializes the results to disk.
It waits for the forked subtasks in the scope.
completionChecks.explanation: Structured concurrency treats forked work as a scoped unit; join waits for the subtasks before results are read.
